prevalence.trend: increasing slowly with aging and obesity trends

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; elevated via cardiovascular and renal complications

vulnerablePopulations: some ethnic minority populations with higher risk
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: FPG ≥126 mg/dL, HbA1c ≥6.5%, 2h OGTT ≥200 mg/dL, or random glucose ≥200 mg/dL with symptoms; per ADA/EASD-aligned criteria

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: risk-based screening common in primary care; varies by member state
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with structured follow-up; varies across countries

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Strong primary care detection and complication screening programs in many countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle + metformin; early use of SGLT2i/GLP-1RA for cardio-renal benefit per ADA/EASD; broad access to modern insulin and monitoring technologies
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Increasing burden with aging and healthcare costs, plus variability across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: High access to modern therapies, multidisciplinary diabetes care, and systematic complication screening
